strategy: Run a full team of Support / Support / Healer / Hitter; the beguile pressure makes soloing unreliable.
Kill the three Tzeltan Cohort minions first (7,440 HP each). They spam Winter Moon and Death Crow, which beguile your wizards, so removing them cuts the crowd control.
Supports spend Rounds 1-3 stacking Potent Feint / TC Feint plus blades (and a prism if the hitter is resisted) while everyone buffs at once. Hit with Myth or Life; she takes +35% from both.
Do not cast a plain Feint on Ixcax; she Feints you back. Use Potent or treasure-card Feints so you still stack roughly +80% and +30% trap value.

Carry Storm Prisms if hitting Storm through her 80% Storm resist, keep a couple of cleanse charms for the Virulent Plague she throws at Death blades, and never stand directly in front of her because of her Enfeeble.

Time the hit before the end of Round 5: at that point she cleanses all traps and prisms off her team and casts a Mass Prism that flips your damage school, so land the kill first or reset your setup after.
Expect a spike whenever anything dies: she casts Berserk on herself and her minions Fortify, so heal through it.
cheatNotes: Whenever any wizard or minion is defeated, Ixcax casts Berserk on herself (more damage and pierce) and the surviving minions cast Fortify (less damage taken), so expect a damage spike right after a kill.
If you place a normal Feint on her she immediately Feints you back; use Potent or treasure-card Feints instead so the retaliation matters less.

At the end of every fifth round she cleanses all negative Wards (your traps) off her team and casts a unique Mass Storm Prism that converts your incoming damage to a random school, so hit before that or re-set afterward.
